summary: Create Postman API collections for LoanFlow 360 REST API
description:
Create and maintain Postman collection files that cover all LoanFlow 360 REST API endpoints documented in <docs/api-reference.md>.

Two collections are required:
- Gateway collection: all requests routed through the API Gateway at a single base URL
- Direct-service collection: each service folder targets its own URL variable, bypassing the gateway

Both collections must include environment files for local development and dev/staging, a README documenting the contribution convention, and the PR template must be updated to enforce that Postman files stay in sync with <docs/api-reference.md>.

All request bodies, example responses, and auth patterns must match the API reference exactly.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Gateway collection covers all documented endpoints
acceptanceCriteria.given: the gateway Postman collection is imported with the local environment selected
acceptanceCriteria.when: a reviewer inspects the collection folders
acceptanceCriteria.then: all endpoints from docs/api-reference.md are present, organised into folders matching the five service sections, with correct HTTP methods, path variables, query params, and example request bodies

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Direct-service collection routes each folder to the correct service
acceptanceCriteria.given: the direct-service collection is imported with the local-direct environment selected
acceptanceCriteria.when: a request is sent from any folder
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request targets the correct per-service URL variable (identityServiceUrl, loanServiceUrl, documentServiceUrl, underwritingServiceUrl, auditServiceUrl) and does not route through the API Gateway

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Login request auto-captures the access token
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Login request is executed and the server returns HTTP 200
acceptanceCriteria.then: the test script stores accessToken and refreshToken as collection variables, and all subsequent authenticated requests in the same session use the captured token automatically

acceptanceCriteria.given: a developer opens a pull request that adds or changes an API endpoint
acceptanceCriteria.when: they review the Author Checklist in the PR template
acceptanceCriteria.then: a checklist item requires both docs/api-reference.md and the Postman collection files in docs/postman/ to be updated in the same PR

tasks.description: Create <docs/postman/loanflow-360.postman_collection.json> covering all endpoints from <docs/api-reference.md>. Organise into five service folders. Set collection-level bearer auth using {{accessToken}}. Add test scripts on Login and Create Application to auto-capture accessToken and applicationId.

Create <docs/postman/loanflow-360.direct-services.postman_collection.json> with per-service URL variables (identityServiceUrl, loanServiceUrl, documentServiceUrl, underwritingServiceUrl, auditServiceUrl). Add a Health Check request to each folder. Mirror all requests from the gateway collection.

tasks.summary: Create environment files for both collections
tasks.description:
Create four environment files in <docs/postman/>:
- loanflow-360.local.postman_environment.json (gateway, localhost:8080)
- loanflow-360.dev.postman_environment.json (gateway, dev hostname)
- loanflow-360.local-direct.postman_environment.json (direct, localhost:8081–8085)
- loanflow-360.dev-direct.postman_environment.json (direct, dev hostnames)

Mark loginPassword as type secret in all environments.

Create <docs/postman/README.md> documenting: file inventory, quickstart import steps, environment variable reference table, folder-to-service mapping, and step-by-step instructions for adding or editing an endpoint in both collections.

Add a checklist item to <.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md> Author Checklist requiring that docs/api-reference.md and the Postman collections in docs/postman/ are updated in the same PR when an endpoint is added or changed. Reference docs/postman/README.md.
